name: pydid

metadata.name: pydid
metadata.summary: Python library for validating, constructing, and representing DIDs and DID Documents

documentation:
# PyDID

[![pypi release](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/pydid)](https://pypi.org/project/pydid/)
[![pre-commit](https://img.shields.io/badge/pre--commit-enabled-brightgreen?logo=pre-commit&logoColor=white)](https://github.com/pre-commit/pre-commit)

Python library for validating, constructing, and representing DIDs and DID Documents.

## Installation

Using a virtual environment is generally recommended:

```sh
python -m venv env
source env/bin/activate
```

Install with pip:

```sh
pip install pydid
```

## Development

This project is managed with [Poetry](https://python-poetry.org/).

To begin making code changes, clone this repo and do the following to install
dependencies:

```sh
python -m venv env
source env/bin/activate
pip install poetry
poetry install
```

## Contributing

See [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md).

W3C DID Representation.

modules.name: pydid.doc.generic
documentation:
Generic DID Document Classes.

If using Python 3.7+, these can be used to simplify subclassing a DID Document.
These classes are experimental and subject to change without notice. Use at
your own risk.

functions.name: pydid.doc.corrections.insert_missing_ids
documentation:
Insert missing resource IDs.

This correction can be applied directly to a raw document dictionary or
with the pydid.deserialize_document method:

```pycon
>>> import pydid
>>> doc_raw = {"@context": "http://example.com", "id": "did:example:123"}
>>> doc_raw = pydid.corrections.insert_missing_ids(doc_raw)
```

Or:
\>>> import pydid
\>>> doc_raw = {"@context": "<http://example.com>", "id": "did:example:123"}
\>>> doc = pydid.deserialize_document(
... doc_raw, corrections=[pydid.corrections.insert_missing_ids]
... )

functions.name: pydid.doc.corrections.public_key_is_verification_method
documentation:
Transform publicKey to verificationMethod.

This is helpful if dealing with DID resolvers that have not updated to the
latest DID Spec.

This correction can be applied directly to a raw document dictionary or
with the pydid.deserialize_document method:

```pycon
>>> import pydid
>>> doc_raw = {
...     "@context": "http://example.com",
...     "id": "did:example:123",
...     "publicKey": [{"id": "did:example:123#key-1"}],
... }
>>> doc_raw = pydid.corrections.public_key_is_verification_method(doc_raw)
>>> assert "verificationMethod" in doc_raw
>>> assert "publicKey" not in doc_raw
```

Or:
\>>> import pydid
\>>> doc_raw = {
... "@context": "<http://example.com>",
... "id": "did:example:123",
... "publicKey": [{"id": "did:example:123#key-1"}],
... }
\>>> doc = pydid.deserialize_document(
... doc_raw, corrections=[pydid.corrections.public_key_is_verification_method]
... )
\>>> assert doc.verification_method

methods.name: pydid.doc.doc.BasicDIDDocument._index_resources
documentation:
Index resources by ID.

IDs are not guaranteed to be unique within the document.
The first instance is stored in the index and subsequent id collisions
are checked against the original. If they do not match, an error will
be thrown.

methods.name: pydid.doc.doc.BasicDIDDocument.dereference
documentation: Dereference a DID URL to a document resource.

classes.name: pydid.doc.doc.DIDDocument

methods.name: pydid.doc.doc.DIDDocument.deserialize
documentation: Wrap deserialization with a basic validation pass before matching to type.

DID Document for DID Spec version 1.0.

Registered verification method and service types are parsed into specific objects.

classes.name: pydid.doc.doc.NonconformantDocument

methods.name: pydid.doc.doc.NonconformantDocument._index_resources
documentation:
Index resources by ID.

This is done in the most permissive way possible. ID collisions will
result in overwritten data instead of raising an error.

methods.name: pydid.doc.doc.NonconformantDocument.dereference
documentation: Dereference a DID URL to a document resource.

Container for non-conformant documents.

This container allows us to interact with these documents without going
through the more rigorous validation.
